concept.title: What Is Intelligent Content?
concept.shortdesc: Intelligent content is structurally rich, semantically categorized, and machine-readable — enabling AI systems to discover, classify, retrieve, and deliver it without human intervention.

section.title: How is intelligent content defined?
section.p: Intelligent content has five defining characteristics that distinguish it from ordinary documentation:

ol.li: Structurally rich: Content is organized into typed, modular units (concepts, tasks, references) with semantic markup — not flat text with visual formatting.
Semantically categorized: Every content unit carries metadata that describes what it is about, who it is for, how difficult it is, and what user intent it serves.
Discoverable: Metadata, taxonomy, and controlled vocabulary enable precise search, filtering, and retrieval — by both humans and AI systems.
Reusable: Content units are authored once and assembled into multiple outputs, audiences, and delivery channels without duplication.
Adaptable: Conditional processing and metadata-driven filtering enable personalized delivery based on user role, platform, product version, or context.

section.p: When content has these properties, AI systems — chatbots, search engines, recommendation engines, and RAG pipelines — can work with it effectively. Without them, AI is limited to brute-force keyword matching on unstructured text.

section.title: Why is DITA the ideal foundation for intelligent content?
section.p: DITA (Darwin Information Typing Architecture) was designed from the ground up for the properties that intelligent content requires:

row.entry: Property
How DITA provides it
How AI uses it
Structural typing
<concept>, <task>, <reference> — each with specific child elements
AI can format answers differently: prose for concepts, step-lists for tasks, tables for references
Prolog metadata
<prolog> with audience, category, difficulty, dates, custom <data> elements
Enables metadata-filtered retrieval: "show me advanced developer content about XSLT"
Section granularity
ID'd <section> elements with titles inside topic bodies
Section-aware chunking produces focused, retrievable units for vector search
Controlled vocabulary
Subject scheme maps with <subjectdef>
Normalizes classification values — AI knows "developer" and "engineer" mean the same audience
Content reuse
Conref, keyref, conkeyref mechanisms
Single-source truth — AI retrieves the authoritative version, not duplicates
Conditional processing
DITAVAL filtering on audience, platform, product attributes
AI can serve role-specific or product-specific content variants

section.title: The shift from documents to content components
section.p: The fundamental shift in intelligent content is moving from a document-centric model to a component-centric model:

row.entry: Document-centric (Word/flat XML/PDF)
Component-centric (DITA)
A "file" is the unit of authoring
A "topic" is the unit of authoring — typed by purpose
Content is organized by document structure
Content is organized by information model and assembled by maps
Formatting is embedded in content
Formatting is applied at publish time via transforms
Metadata is an afterthought (YAML front matter at best)
Metadata is a first-class citizen in the prolog
Reuse requires copy-paste
Reuse is built into the architecture (conref, keyref)
One output per document
One source, many outputs (HTML5, PDF, JSON, chatbot)
